title: Add light/dark theme toggle and OCR 'Coming Soon' message

requirements.summary: Add a light/dark theme toggle button in the app header that persists user preference and works with existing ThemeProvider
acceptanceCriteria: A theme toggle button (sun/moon icon) is visible in the top-right corner of the app on all pages
Clicking the button switches the app between light and dark themes
The active theme is persisted in localStorage under 'acadmind_theme'
On page reload, the previously selected theme is restored automatically
Dark mode applies the dark CSS custom properties defined in frontend/src/index.css consistently across all pages and components
The toggle button itself is styled to remain visible and accessible in both light and dark themes

file_operations.description: Create a new ThemeToggle component that renders a button with sun/moon icons (using lucide-react icons). The component reads the current theme from a theme context or hook, toggles between 'light' and 'dark' on click, persists the selection to localStorage under key 'acadmind_theme', and updates the document root element's class or data attribute to trigger CSS custom property changes. Style the button to remain visible in both themes using appropriate foreground/background tokens from frontend/src/index.css.

Create a ThemeContext and ThemeProvider that manages theme state ('light' or 'dark'), reads the initial theme from localStorage key 'acadmind_theme' (defaulting to 'light' if not set), applies the theme to the document root element (via className or dataset), and exposes a toggleTheme function and current theme value via a useTheme hook. Ensure the provider initializes theme on mount and synchronizes with localStorage on every change.

Wrap the existing app tree with the new ThemeProvider from frontend/src/contexts/ThemeContext.tsx, placing it at the outermost level (inside QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider but outside the Router). Ensure the ThemeProvider is active before any UI renders so the theme is applied immediately on load.

Import and render the ThemeToggle component in the top-right area of the header section, positioned next to or above the hamburger menu button. Ensure the toggle button is visible on all pages and styled to remain accessible in both light and dark themes. Adjust layout/spacing as needed to accommodate the new button without disrupting existing navigation elements.

requirements.summary: Replace negative/error OCR result text with a 'Coming Soon' message on the TimetableUpload page
acceptanceCriteria: When the user clicks the 'Extract Timetable' button on the OCR Upload page, no 'No' or negative error text is shown
Instead, a 'Coming Soon' message is displayed inline in the result area
The 'Coming Soon' message is clearly readable and styled consistently with the app theme
The loading spinner (if shown) still appears briefly before the 'Coming Soon' message is displayed
No other functionality on the TimetableUpload page is altered

file_operations.description: Locate the code that handles the OCR extraction result (likely after calling mockOcr or displaying an error) and replace any negative/error message text (e.g., 'No', 'Failed', or error strings) with a clearly styled 'Coming Soon' message. Display this message inline in the result area where the extracted slots or error would normally appear. Preserve the loading spinner behavior and ensure the 'Coming Soon' text is styled using theme-aware Tailwind classes (e.g., text-foreground, bg-muted) so it remains visible in both light and dark modes. Do not alter file upload, slot editing, or save functionality.
